headline: 08 June 2026
subtitle: MOU Stalemate Continues; Oil Gap on Monday Open; Second Clean Window for Kuwait

metrics.metric: Days since last confirmed cyber incident (Gulf FS)

items.text: No air raid sirens, civil defense alerts, shelter-in-place orders, or emergency broadcasts issued in Kuwait during the coverage window (07 June 07:00 UTC+3 – 08 June 07:00 UTC+3). Kuwait MOI issued no new emergency advisory. The last confirmed siren activation was 06 June 04:15 local, outside this window. Staff should continue monitoring @MOIKuwait and KUNA before travelling to the office.

items.bold_heading: No confirmed strikes on Kuwait or GCC territory in coverage window
items.text: No confirmed missile or drone strikes on Kuwait or GCC territory within the coverage window (07 June 07:00 UTC+3 – 08 June 07:00 UTC+3). This is the second consecutive clean 24-hour window for Kuwait.

items.bold_heading: NAVCENT downed 2 Iranian drones, Strait of Hormuz, 07 June
items.text: US forces conducted counter-drone operations in the Strait of Hormuz on 07 June, downing 2 Iranian drones assessed as posing a threat to regional maritime traffic. Consistent with NAVCENT's public operations notice issued Jun 1. These are US self-defence operations, not inbound attacks on GCC territory.

items.bold_heading: IRGC Bahrain (Juffair) naval claims — SINGLE SOURCE, unconfirmed by CENTCOM
items.text: IRGC claims from Jun 6 regarding missile, drone, and naval strikes against US Fifth Fleet facilities in Bahrain remain unconfirmed by CENTCOM as of compilation. Treat as [SINGLE SOURCE — IRGC statement only].
No new civil defense alerts or emergency broadcasts in coverage window. Kuwait remains in cautious normal posture.

items.bold_heading: KWI: T4 + T5 open; T1 closed
items.text: Kuwait International Airport T4 (Kuwait Airways) and T5 (Jazeera Airways) operational; T1 closed pending repairs from Jun 3 IRGC drone strike. No DGCA timeline for T1 repair or return of foreign carriers. Verify individual flight status before travel.

items.bold_heading: Civil service: full attendance, reduced summer hours
items.text: Kuwait civil service operating at full attendance with reduced 6-hour workday (Jun 1–Aug 31, 2026). The March–April 30% attendance restriction has been lifted.

items.bold_heading: Land borders and ports: no new disruptions
items.text: Land borders (Saudi Arabia, Iraq) and seaports (Shuwaikh, Shuaiba) operational at ISPS Level 2. Air freight via T1 remains unavailable; perishables/pharma supply tightness continues.
Kuwait-Iran diplomatic downgrade remains in force; two Iranian diplomats expelled with no reversal. Compliance screening for Iranian-linked counterparties should remain current.

MOU negotiations deadlocked: Trump sent draft back to Iran over Jun 7–8 weekend demanding clearer nuclear commitment timing and immediate Hormuz handover; Iran's top negotiator Ghalibaf stated Tehran 'takes concessions through missiles, not dialogue.'

Pakistan's Interior Minister Naqvi delivered dual letters (PM Sharif + Army Chief Munir) to Iran FM Araghchi for Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei on 07 June, in a fresh mediation push via civilian and military tracks simultaneously.
Oil markets opened sharply higher Monday (Brent ~$95.67, WTI ~$93.63) after MOU non-signing weekend; NAVCENT Hormuz operations remain active, sustaining structurally elevated escalation risk.

items.bold_heading: Oil gap risk: Brent ~$95.67 Monday open
items.text: Institutions with mark-to-market energy exposures, oil-linked collateral, or energy-sector credit portfolios should review weekend gap against stress scenarios; MOU collapse or fresh escalation could push Brent above $100 intraday.

items.bold_heading: BCP: second clean window but no stand-down
items.text: MOU remains unsigned; NAVCENT Hormuz operations are active and could trigger a new IRGC retaliation cycle. Full BCP stand-down requires signed MOU AND formal Kuwait MOI all-clear. Neither is met.

items.bold_heading: Cyber retaliation window: post-Jun 5 closed; new cycle risk from Jun 7 NAVCENT engagements
items.text: NAVCENT's Jun 7 counter-drone operations may trigger hacktivist DDoS/defacement waves within 24–72h. Monitor CENTCOM press releases for new strike announcements; each triggers an elevated cyber window.

items.bold_heading: IRGC Bahrain claim (SINGLE SOURCE): watch for CENTCOM confirmation
items.text: If CENTCOM confirms IRGC strike on NSA Bahrain/Juffair, trigger immediate review of correspondent banking and payment intermediaries domiciled in Bahrain.

items.bold_heading: Plan for extended elevated posture through mid-June
items.text: Given Trump's amendment demands and Iran's sanctions-first sequencing insistence, rapid MOU resolution before Jun 15 appears unlikely. Budget all elevated BCP, insurance, and cyber postures for at least two more weeks.

items.bold_heading: JWC JWLA-033 unchanged; WRP 1.5–3% hull value
items.text: Entire Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz, Gulf of Oman, and northern Arabian Sea remain Listed Areas. NAVCENT Jun 7 operations reinforce risk basis; no WRP reduction credible before signed MOU. APCs of $150,000–$400,000 per vessel per call remain in effect.

items.bold_heading: Monday oil gap increases cargo valuation exposure
items.text: Brent +$2.17 weekend gap may create underinsurance risk for undeclared or stale valuations on in-transit Gulf cargoes. Review declared values in open cargo policies for Gulf-routed shipments.

items.bold_heading: KWI T1 aviation war risk: LMA watch active
items.text: Aviation war risk policies activated by Jun 3 KWI T1 strike remain in force. No new LMA advisory issued for Jun 7 NAVCENT Hormuz operations. Seek written confirmation of war exclusion clause applicability for cargo in transit at T1 during Jun 3 event.

articles.title: U.S.-Iran Distrust Holds Up an Agreement
articles.author: Gaby Tejeda
articles.summary: Details how mutual distrust over MOU sequencing — US demanding clearer nuclear commitment timing and immediate Hormuz handover before sanctions relief; Iran insisting on front-loaded sanctions release — is stalling the deal despite both sides claiming near-completion; maps specific sticking points including HEU disposal, proposed $300bn reconstruction fund, and Mojtaba Khamenei's ambiguous decision-making authority.
articles.relevance: The specific unresolved sequencing issues (HEU timing, sanctions, Hormuz control triggers) are the exact tripwires that will determine when BCP de-escalation, WRP normalisation, and oil hedges can safely begin.

articles.title: Iran's New Grand Strategy
articles.author: Narges Bajoghli and Vali Nasr
articles.summary: Argues Iran deliberately applied dispersed missile salvos and drone swarms to deplete interceptor stockpiles while weaponising Hormuz closure as a strategic deterrent designed to outlast US political will; assesses Iranian social cohesion has strengthened rather than collapsed under bombardment, contrary to US assumptions.
articles.relevance: If Hormuz leverage is a permanent Iranian strategic asset rather than a temporary bargaining chip, war-risk premia and BCP postures cannot revert to pre-conflict baselines even after an MOU is signed.

items.bold_heading: MuddyWater/Seedworm phishing lure refresh — Day 101 and MOU stalemate hooks
items.text: Operation Olalampo continues; lure themes update within 24–48h of major news events. Day 101 milestone and MOU weekend non-signing are high-credibility phishing vectors. DLL side-loading and MS Teams external federation remain primary attack vectors.
items.recommended_action: Issue staff awareness alert today incorporating MOU-stalemate and Day 101 lures; block unknown external Teams federation tenants.

items.bold_heading: Iran-linked hacktivist clusters on elevated alert; Jun 7 NAVCENT ops may trigger new wave
items.text: 313 Team, ALTOUFAN TEAM, DieNet, Hydro Kitten remain on high-tempo post-kinetic alert posture. NAVCENT's Jun 7 drone engagements may re-trigger a hacktivist DDoS wave within 24–72h targeting GCC financial services portals and e-payment platforms.
items.recommended_action: Maintain DDoS mitigation hot standby and WAF elevated sensitivity; confirm ISP escalation paths are live for at least 48h after any new CENTCOM strike announcement.

items.bold_heading: CISA AA26-097a OT/ICS advisory: Gulf energy and utility operators at elevated risk
items.text: Iranian-affiliated APT actors have disrupted PLCs across government, water/wastewater, and energy sectors since March 2026. MOU stalemate extension raises probability of OT-adjacent targeting to demonstrate leverage.
items.recommended_action: Verify OT/ICS network segmentation; confirm PLC firmware patching; escalate any anomalous OT alerts immediately.

items.bold_heading: Iran-linked Gulf cyber surge 8× baseline — shift to complex breaches and pre-positioning
items.text: UAE Cybersecurity chief reported 600,000 attacks since late February across ransomware, data breaches, targeted leaks, wipers, and defacements; banking and telecom face both symbolic and operationally critical targeting. MOU stalemate extends the high-activity window indefinitely.
items.threat_actor: IRGC-coordinated APTs and hacktivist clusters (Seedworm, APT42, 313 Team, ALTOUFAN)

items.bold_heading: APT42 cloud persistence via TAMECAT — Gulf FS cloud platforms at MODERATE-HIGH risk
items.text: APT42 now focuses on long-term personal engagement before compromising Microsoft 365/Google Workspace accounts, then establishing persistent backdoor footholds. Financial institutions using cloud-hosted productivity platforms face elevated lateral movement risk after initial credential compromise.

items.bold_heading: Chaos ransomware-as-cover destructive risk — war exclusion clause exposure rising
items.text: IRGC-affiliated actors deploy destructive intrusions disguised as ransomware, potentially triggering cyber insurance war-exclusion clauses and leaving institutions uncompensated. Probability increases with each escalation cycle and each failed MOU deadline.
Iran's formal response to Trump's amended MOU draft
items.why_it_matters: A collapse triggers kinetic/cyber escalation, oil spike above $100, and expanded air raid risk for Kuwait; an acceptance starts the Hormuz mine-clearance clock and de-escalation path.

items.text: NAVCENT Hormuz operations and IRGC counter-response watch
items.why_it_matters: Jun 7 counter-drone engagements may trigger Iran-linked cyber retaliation (DDoS/defacement) within 24–72h; monitor CENTCOM press releases and SocRadar dashboard.

items.text: CENTCOM confirmation or denial of Jun 6 IRGC claims against Fifth Fleet Juffair (Bahrain)
items.why_it_matters: Confirmation elevates risk for Bahrain-domiciled correspondent banking relationships and may trigger fresh JWC listing review for Bahrain-specific exclusions.

NAVCENT drone engagements confirmed; IRGC Bahrain claims SINGLE SOURCE
Consistent across Tier 1 Gulf media; CSC update corroborated

Analysis derived from confirmed Tier 1 facts

Pattern-based; no confirmed new incident this window
